Market prices of important means of production in circulation refer to the wholesale prices and selling prices of enterprises trading important means of production. Different from producer prices, market prices of means of production include producer prices, as well as circulation expenses, profits, and taxes of the enterprises. Producer prices and market prices influence each other. Due to a time lag, their movements may show divergence in a certain period of time. The monitoring of market prices of important means of production in circulation includes the prices of 50 kinds of products under 9 categories. For details of product categories and specifications, please see the attached table. The monitoring covers nearly 2,000 wholesalers, agents, and dealers of more than 300 trading markets in 31 provinces (autonomous regions and municipalities). Price monitoring methods include information officers gathering prices on-site or requesting prices by telephone, instant messaging tools, e-mail, and so on. The number of products with price increases, decreases, and no change is counted based on the percentage change (%). articles.title: 2.Purchasing Managersâ Index for June 2026
articles.extracted_text: Department of Service Statistics of NBS of China China Federation of Logistics & Purchasing (CFLP) I. Manufacturing Purchasing Managersâ Index In June, the purchasing managersâ index (PMI) of Chinaâs manufacturing industry was 50.3%, an increase of 0.3 percentage points from the previous month and back to the expansionary range. By enterprise size, the PMI for large-sized enterprises was 50.7%, a decrease of 0.4 percentage points from the previous month, which was above the threshold; the PMI for medium sized enterprises was 50.5%, an increase of 1.9 percentage points from the previous month, which was above the threshold; the PMI for small-sized enterprises was 48.2%, a decrease of 0.3 percentage points from the previous month, which was below the threshold. By sub-index, among the five sub-indexes that constitute the manufacturing PMI, the production index and the new order index were both above the threshold, while the raw materials inventory index, the employment index and the supplier delivery time index were all below the threshold. The production index was 51.4%, an increase of 0.2 percentage points from the previous month, indicating an accelerated expansion in production activities in the manufacturing industry. The new order index was 51.2%, an increase of 1.3 percentage points from the previous month, indicating a recovery in the market demand of the manufacturing industry. The raw materials inventory index was 48.4%, a decrease of 0.2 percentage points from the previous month, indicating a continued decline in the inventories of main raw materials in the manufacturing industry. The employment index was 48.5%, a decrease of 0.1 percentage points from the previous month, indicating a slight decline in the employment climate of the manufacturing industry. The supplier delivery time index was 49.9%, an increase of 0.7 percentage points from the previous month, and still below the threshold, indicating a slight slowdown in raw material delivery times among manufacturing suppliers. By industry, the business activity index for the construction industry was 49.0%, an increase of 0.2 percentage points from the previous month; that for the service industry was 50.4%, an increase of 0.1 percentage points from the previous month. In terms of the service industry, the business activity indexes for telecommunication, radio, television and satellite transmission services, Internet software and information technology services, monetary and financial services and insurance were all in the relatively high climate range of above 55.0%; those for civil aviation transportation and real estate were all below the threshold. The new order index was 48.0%, an increase of 3.0 percentage points from the previous month, indicating a significant improvement in the market demand climate of the non-manufacturing industry. articles.title: 3.Report on the Development of Culture and Related Industries in 2025
articles.extracted_text: In 2025, under the strong leadership of the Party Central Committee with Comrade Xi Jinping at its core, all regions and departments fully implemented the decisions and plans of the Party Central Committee and the State Council. Centered on the goal of building China into a country with a strong culture, the country moved faster to nurture new-type cultural enterprises and new cultural forms, optimized the business environment, and improved the quality of development. Cultural and related industries achieved vigorous growth with steadily rising profitability. Industries with new cultural forms took the lead in driving development, and scientific and technological innovation in cultural industries picked up speed. i. Business revenue of culture and related industries exceeded 20 trillion yuan With the continuous improvement of Chinaâs cultural industrial system and market system, Chinaâs culture and related industries (hereinafter referred to as âcultural industriesâ) have expanded steadily. In 2025, cultural industries nationwide achieved a business revenue of 20,825.4 billion yuan [1] , surpassing the 20 trillion yuan threshold and hitting a record high, up by 8.8% year on year [2] . Underpinned by culture-related services, the structure of cultural industries kept improving. In 2025, culture-related services achieved a business revenue of 12,305.2 billion yuan, up by 12.8% year on year, accounting for 59.1% of the total business revenue of cultural industries, up by 2.1 percentage points year on year. Culture-related services contributed 82.7% to the business revenue growth of cultural industries. Culture-related manufacturing and culture-related wholesale and retail achieved business revenue of 4,991.9 billion yuan and 3,528.3 billion yuan, respectively, up by 0.6% and 8.0% year on year. ii. Business revenue of nine major sectors of cultural industries achieved growth across the board In 2025, business revenue saw growth across nine major sectors of cultural industries. Specifically, four sectors posted double-digit business revenue growth year on year: creative design services (13.2%), content creation and production (12.4%), news and information services (12.3%), and cultural, entertainment and leisure services (10.7%). The other five sectors recorded year-on-year growth rates as follows: 8.0% in cultural equipment production, 7.7% in cultural communication channels, 3.1% in cultural investment and operation, 2.7% in cultural auxiliary production and intermediary services, and 2.5% in cultural consumption terminal production. By region, cultural industries in eastern and western regions achieved business revenue of 15,528.0 billion yuan and 2,029.8 billion yuan, up by 9.5% and 9.9% year on year, respectively, both higher than the national average growth rate. The central and northeastern regions achieved business revenue of 3,007.1 billion yuan and 260.5 billion yuan, up by 4.7% and 4.8% year on year. i. Total assets of cultural industries kept rising At the end of 2025, the total assets of Chinaâs cultural industries reached 37,447.4 billion yuan, an increase of 8.9% over the end of the previous year. By industry type, total assets of culture-related manufacturing, culture-related wholesale and retail, and culture-related services were 5,060.2 billion yuan, 2,744.3 billion yuan and 29,642.8 billion yuan, respectively, up by 5.7%, 7.2% and 9.6% over the end of the previous year. articles.title: 4.Profits of Industrial Enterprises above the Designated Size from January to May in 2026
articles.extracted_text: From January to May, the total profits of industrial enterprises above the designated size nationwide reached 3,143.96 billion yuan, a year-on-year increase of 18.8% (calculated on a comparable basis, see Annotation 2 for details). From January to May, among industrial enterprises above the designated size, state-holding enterprises realized a total profit of 1,048.66 billion yuan, a year-on-year increase of 19.6%; share-holding enterprises realized a total profit of 2,434.81 billion yuan, an increase of 24.1%; enterprises funded by foreign investors and investors from Hong Kong, Macao and Taiwan realized a total profit of 695.72 billion yuan, an increase of 4.2%; private enterprises realized a total profit of 772.65 billion yuan, an increase of 10.7%. From January to May, the mining industry realized a total profit of 479.52 billion yuan, a year-on-year increase of 33.5%; the manufacturing industry realized a total profit of 2,328.52 billion yuan, an increase of 20.0%; the production and supply of electricity, heat power, gas and water realized a total profit of 335.92 billion yuan, a decrease of 2.7%. From January to May, the profits of main industries were as follows: the profit of the smelting and pressing of non-ferrous metals increased by 117.1% year on year, that of the manufacture of computers, communication equipment and other electronic equipment increased by 103.9%, that of the manufacture of raw chemical materials and chemical products increased by 71.6%, that of the mining and washing of coal increased by 33.5%, that of the extraction of petroleum and natural gas increased by 17.2%, that of the textile industry increased by 11.7%, that of the manufacture of general-purpose machinery decreased by 0.2%, that of the production and supply of electricity and heat power decreased by 4.1%, that of the manufacture of special-purpose machinery decreased by 5.5%, that of the processing of food from agricultural and sideline products decreased by 13.3%, that of the manufacture of electrical machinery and apparatus decreased by 13.7%, that of the manufacture of automobiles decreased by 19.8%, that of the smelting and pressing of ferrous metals decreased by 37.4%, that of the manufacture of non-metallic mineral products decreased by 48.9%, and the processing of petroleum, coal and other fuels turned from loss to profit. From January to May, industrial enterprises above the designated size achieved a business revenue of 56.55 trillion yuan, a year-on-year increase of 5.5%; the operating costs incurred were 48.04 trillion yuan, an increase of 4.7%; the profit rate of business revenue was 5.56%, a year-on-year increase of 0.63 percentage points. At the end of May, the assets of industrial enterprises above the designated size totaled 193.55 trillion yuan, a year-on-year increase of 5.8%; the liabilities totaled 112.69 trillion yuan, an increase of 6.5%; the ownersâ equity totaled 80.87 trillion yuan, an increase of 5.0%; the asset-liability ratio was 58.2%, a year-on-year increase of 0.3 percentage points. At the end of May, the accounts receivable of industrial enterprises above the designated size totaled 28.17 trillion yuan, a year-on-year increase of 7.7%; the inventory of finished goods was 7.14 trillion yuan, an increase of 8.8%. articles.title: 6.Industrial Production Operation in May 2026
articles.extracted_text: In May, the total value added of industrial enterprises above the designated size increased by 4.5% year on year in real terms (the growth rates of value added are the real growth rates after deducting price factors), 0.4 percentage points higher than the previous month. On a month-on-month basis, in May, the total value added of industrial enterprises above the designated size increased by 0.40% over the previous month. From January to May, the total value added of industrial enterprises above the designated size increased by 5.4% year on year. In terms of three sectors, in May, the value added of the mining industry increased by 2.3% year on year, that of the manufacturing industry increased by 4.4%, and that of the production and supply of electricity, heat power, gas and water increased by 7.6%. In terms of economic types, in May, the value added of state-holding enterprises increased by 3.7% year on year; that of share-holding enterprises increased by 5.2%; that of enterprises funded by foreign investors and investors from Hong Kong, Macao, and Taiwan increased by 1.9%; and that of private enterprises increased by 2.7%. In terms of industries, in May, the value added of 28 out of 41 major industries continued to show year-on-year growth. Among them, the value added of the mining and washing of coal went up by 3.5%, that of the extraction of petroleum and natural gas up by 1.5%, that of the processing of food from agricultural and sideline products up by 1.5%, that of the manufacture of liquor, beverages and refined tea down by 2.7%, that of the textile industry up by 2.6%, that of the manufacture of raw chemical materials and chemical products up by 0.3%, that of the manufacture of non-metallic mineral products down by 5.6%, that of the smelting and pressing of ferrous metals up by 1.6%, that of the smelting and pressing of non-ferrous metals down by 4.5%, that of the manufacture of general-purpose machinery up by 6.7%, that of the manufacture of special-purpose machinery up by 9.1%, that of the manufacture of automobiles up by 8.3%, that of the manufacture of railway, ship, aerospace and other transport equipment up by 7.4%, that of the manufacture of electrical machinery and apparatus up by 4.7%, that of the manufacture of computers, communication equipment and other electronic equipment up by 17.0%, and that of the production and supply of electricity and heat power up by 8.7%. In terms of products, in May, the output of 300 out of 626 products produced by industrial enterprises above the designated size increased year on year. Among them, the output of rolled steel was 123.03 million tons, down by 2.8% year on year; that of cement was 149.91 million tons, down by 8.1%; that of ten kinds of non-ferrous metals was 6.98 million tons, up by 2.2%; that of ethylene was 3.38 million tons, up by 2.1%; that of motor vehicles was 2.582 million sets, down by 3.2%, including 1.489 million new energy vehicles, up by 17.8%; the electricity generation was 784.3 billion kWh, up by 4.2%; and the crude oil processing volume was 53.72 million tons, down by 9.1%. In May, the product sales ratio of industrial enterprises above the designated size was 96.0%, a year-on-year decrease of 0.1 percentage points; and the export delivery value realized by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 1,388.4 billion yuan, a nominal increase of 10.1% year on year. Industrial Production Operation in May 2026 Value Added of Industrial Enterprises Above

articles.title: 7.Investment in Fixed Assets from January to May 2026
articles.extracted_text: From January to May 2026, the national investment in fixed assets (excluding rural households) was 17,851.2 billion yuan, a year-on-year decrease of 4.1% (calculated on a comparable basis, see Annotation 7 for details). Specifically, the investment in intellectual property products increased by 9.3% year on year. By industry, the investment in the primary industry was 388.7 billion yuan, a year-on-year increase of 5.9%; the investment in the secondary industry was 6,498.9 billion yuan, up 0.1%; and the investment in the tertiary industry was 10,963.6 billion yuan, down 6.8%. The industrial investment increased by 0.1% year on year. Specifically, the investment in mining increased by 6.2%, the investment in manufacturing decreased by 0.4%, and the investment in production and supply of electricity, heat power, gas and water decreased by 0.1%. The investment in infrastructure (see Annotation 1 for details of statistical scope) increased by 0.6% year on year. Specifically, the investment in waterway transportation increased by 23.3%, the investment in civil aviation transportation increased by 21.7%, the investment in ecological conservation and environmental governance increased by 3.5%, and the investment in the production and supply of gas increased by 2.8%. By region, the investment in the eastern region decreased by 6.4% year on year, the investment in the central region decreased by 2.9%, the investment in the western region decreased by 6.6%, and the investment in the northeastern region decreased by 17.5%. By statistical category of registration, the investment in fixed assets of domestic-invested enterprises decreased by 3.9% year on year, the investment in fixed assets of enterprises funded by investors from Hong Kong, Macao, and Taiwan decreased by 8.7%, and the investment in fixed assets of foreign-invested enterprises decreased by 4.3%. From January to May 2026, non-governmental investment in fixed assets decreased by 7.1% year on year. On a month-on-month basis, the investment in fixed assets (excluding rural households) decreased by 1.91% in May. Investment in Fixed Assets (Excluding Rural Households) from January to May 2026 Investment in Fixed Assets (Excluding Rural Households) Agriculture, forestry, animal husbandry and fishery Of which: Processing of food from agricultural and sideline products Manufacture of raw chemical materials and chemical products Smelting and pressing of non-ferrous metals Manufacture of railway, ship, aerospace and other transport equipment Manufacture of electrical machinery and apparatus Manufacture of computers, communication equipment and other electronic equipment Production and supply of electricity, heat power, gas and water Water conservancy, environment and public facilities management Of which: Management of water conservancy Enterprises funded by investors from Hong Kong, Macao, and Taiwan Note: The growth rates in this table are not adjusted for price factors. articles.title: 8.Investment in Real Estate Development from January to May 2026
articles.extracted_text: I. The Completion of Investment in Real Estate Development From January to May, the investment in real estate development was 3,035.6 billion yuan, a year-on-year decrease of 16.2% (calculated on a comparable basis, see Annotation 5 for details), of which the investment in residential buildings was 2,342.6 billion yuan, down 15.6%. From January to May, the floor space of buildings under construction by real estate development enterprises was 5,487.75 million square meters, a year-on-year decrease of 12.3%. Specifically, the floor space of residential buildings under construction was 3,808.30 million square meters, down 12.6%. The floor space of buildings newly started was 179.29 million square meters, down 22.6%. Specifically, the floor space of residential buildings newly started was 130.84 million square meters, down 23.4%. The floor space of buildings completed was 140.87 million square meters, down 23.4%. Specifically, the floor space of residential buildings completed was 99.99 million square meters, down 25.0%. II. Sales of Newly Built Commercial Buildings From January to May, the floor space of newly built commercial buildings sold was 313.20 million square meters, a year-on-year decrease of 10.8%, of which the floor space of residential buildings sold decreased by 12.1%. The sales of newly built commercial buildings were 2,936.6 billion yuan, down 13.5%, of which the sales of residential buildings decreased by 14.1%. At the end of May, the floor space of commercial buildings for sale was 771.82 million square meters, down 0.4% year on year. Specifically, the floor space for sale held for less than three years was 571.52 million square meters, down 2.8%. III. Funds for Investment This Year for Real Estate Development Enterprises From January to May, the funds for investment this year for real estate development enterprises were 3,275.6 billion yuan, a year-on-year decrease of 19.0%. Among them, domestic loans were 487.5 billion yuan, down 28.7%; self-raised funds were 1,198.5 billion yuan, down 13.0%; deposits and advance receipts were 1,001.2 billion yuan, down 16.1%; individual mortgage loans were 406.6 billion yuan, down 28.0%. articles.title: 9.Total Retail Sales of Consumer Goods from January to May 2026
articles.extracted_text: From January to May, the total retail sales of consumer goods reached 20,603.1 billion yuan, up by 1.4% year on year. Specifically, the retail sales of consumer goods excluding automobiles reached 19,002.2 billion yuan, up by 2.7%. In May, the total retail sales of consumer goods reached 4,109.0 billion yuan, down by 0.6% year on year. Specifically, the retail sales of consumer goods excluding automobiles reached 3,778.1 billion yuan, up by 1.1%. By business area, from January to May, the retail sales of consumer goods in urban areas reached 17,866.2 billion yuan, up by 1.2% year on year; those in rural areas were 2,736.9 billion yuan, up by 2.6%. In May, the retail sales of consumer goods in urban areas reached 3,574.1 billion yuan, down by 0.9% year on year; those in rural areas were 534.9 billion yuan, up by 1.5%. By consumption pattern, from January to May, the retail sales of goods were 18,254.3 billion yuan, up by 1.2% year on year; the income of the catering industry was 2,348.8 billion yuan, up by 3.1%. In May, the retail sales of goods were 3,648.5 billion yuan, down by 0.7% year on year; the income of the catering industry was 460.5 billion yuan, up by 0.6%. By retail format, from January to May, the retail sales of convenience stores and supermarkets among retail enterprises (units) above the designated size increased by 6.8% and 3.6% year on year, respectively; those of specialty stores, department stores and brand-exclusive stores decreased by 1.2%, 1.8% and 7.6%, respectively. From January to May, Chinaâs online retail sales of goods and services reached 8,317.7 billion yuan, up by 5.9% year on year. Specifically, the online retail sales of goods were 5,271.8 billion yuan, up by 5.0%; among the online retail sales of goods, those of food, clothing, and daily necessities increased by 15.5%, 7.2%, and 1.6%, respectively. The online retail sales of services were 3,045.9 billion yuan, up by 7.6%. articles.title: 10.Energy Production in May 2026
articles.extracted_text: In May 2026, the raw coal production by industrial enterprises above the designated size remained at a relatively high level, the growth of crude oil production was steady, the growth of natural gas production fell slightly, and the growth of electricity generation accelerated. I. Raw Coal, Crude Oil and Natural Gas Production and Related Information Raw coal production remained at a relatively high level. In May, the raw coal production by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 400 million tons, down by 1.7% year on year; the average daily output was 12.81 million tons. From January to May, the raw coal production by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 1.98 billion tons, a year-on-year decrease of 0.3%. The growth of crude oil production was steady. In May, the crude oil production by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 18.57 million tons, a year-on-year increase of 0.5%, 0.7 percentage points lower than that in April; the average daily output was 599 thousand tons. From January to May, the crude oil production by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 91.31 million tons, a year-on-year increase of 1.1%. The decline in the processing volume of crude oil expanded. In May, the processing volume of crude oil by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 53.72 million tons, a year-on-year decrease of 9.1%, with the decline expanded by 3.3 percentage points from April; the average daily processing volume was 1.733 million tons. From January to May, the processing volume of crude oil by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 292.80 million tons, a year-on-year decrease of 2.2%. Natural gas production fell slightly. In May, the production of natural gas by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 21.7 billion cubic meters, a year-on-year decrease of 2.2%, compared with a 1.9% increase in April; the average daily output was 700 million cubic meters. From January to May, the production of natural gas by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 111.7 billion cubic meters, a year-on-year increase of 1.7%. Electricity generation by industrial enterprises above the designated size grew faster. In May, electricity generation by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 784.3 billion kWh, a year-on-year increase of 4.2%, with the growth rate 1.6 percentage points higher than that in April; the average daily electricity generation was 25.30 billion kWh. From January to May, electricity generation by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 3,912.9 billion kWh, a year-on-year increase of 3.6%. By category, in May, the growth of thermal power generation by industrial enterprises above the designated size slowed down, while that of hydropower and solar power generation accelerated; the growth of nuclear power and wind power generation turned from decline to growth. Among them, thermal power generation by industrial enterprises above the designated size increased by 2.1% year on year, with the growth rate 1.0 percentage point lower than that in April; hydropower generation increased by 13.0%, with the growth rate 0.8 percentage points higher than that in April; nuclear power generation increased by 5.0%, compared with an 8.7% decline in April; wind power generation increased by 0.5%, compared with a 5.0% decline in April; solar power generation increased by 12.1%, with the growth rate 5.0 percentage points higher than that i

articles.title: 11.National Economy Showed Steady Momentum with Innovation-led and High-quality Developmen...
articles.extracted_text: In May, under the strong leadership of the Central Committee of the Communist Party of China (CPC) with Comrade Xi Jinping at its core, all regions and departments fully implemented the decisions and arrangements made by the CPC Central Committee and the State Council, adhered to the general principle of pursuing progress while ensuring stability, fully and faithfully applied the new development philosophy on all fronts, moved faster to foster a new pattern of development, strictly implemented more proactive and effective macro policies, and effectively addressed external shocks and challenges. As a result, production and supply registered steady progress, employment and prices were generally stable, foreign trade demonstrated strong resilience, and new growth drivers witnessed robust development. The national economy maintained steady momentum with innovation-led and high-quality development. 1. Industrial Production Witnessed Accelerated Growth and Equipment Manufacturing and High-Tech Manufacturing Grew Fast. In May, the total value added of industrial enterprises above the designated size grew by 4.5 percent year on year, 0.4 percentage points faster than that of the previous month; the month-on-month growth was 0.40 percent. In terms of sectors, the value added of mining increased by 2.3 percent year on year, that of manufacturing grew by 4.4 percent, and that of production and supply of electricity, heat power, gas and water went up by 7.6 percent. The value added of equipment manufacturing increased by 9.5 percent year on year and that of high-tech manufacturing increased by 15.1 percent, 1.2 percentage points and 2.3 percentage points faster than that of the previous month respectively. An analysis by types of ownership showed that the value added of state holding enterprises was up by 3.7 percent year on year; that of share-holding enterprises was up by 5.2 percent; that of enterprises funded by foreign investors or investors from Hong Kong, Macao and Taiwan was up by 1.9 percent; and that of private enterprises was up by 2.7 percent. In terms of products, the production of 3D printing devices, lithium-ion batteries and industrial robots grew by 54.4 percent, 40.0 percent and 27.9 percent year on year respectively. In the first five months, the value added of industrial enterprises above the designated size went up by 5.4 percent year on year. In May, the Manufacturing Purchasing Managersâ Index stood at 50.0 percent. The Production and Operation Expectation Index was 53.9 percent. In the first four months, the total profits made by industrial enterprises above the designated size were 2,435.8 billion yuan, up by 18.2 percent year on year. 2. Service Sector Grew Steadily and Modern Services Showed Good Momentum of Development. In May, the Index of Services Production grew by 4.4 percent year on year, 0.1 percentage points faster than that of the previous month. Specifically, that of information transmission, software and information technology services, leasing and business services, finance, and transport, storage and postal services grew by 11.3 percent, 10.9 percent, 7.0 percent and 4.8 percent year on year respectively. In the first five months, the Index of Services Production increased by 4.8 percent year on year. In the first four months, the business revenue of service enterprises above the designated size grew by 6.4 percent year on year. articles.title: Greetings on Sea and River Fleet Workers’ Day
articles.raw_summary: Vladimir Putin extended his greetings to current and veteran sea and river fleet workers on their professional holiday.
articles.extracted_text: All content on this site is licensed under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International Vladimir Putin extended his greetings to current and veteran sea and river fleet workers on their professional holiday. “Water transport is rightfully considered an essential and integral part of the Russian transport system. It contributes to the country’s socioeconomic development, strengthens interregional and international ties, and reliably provides passenger and freight services. Today, amid increasingly complex challenges, the industry faces a number of major and urgent tasks. These include further efforts to renew the fleet, modernise port infrastructure, develop resilient logistics routes and international transport corridors, and expand the use of advanced shipping technologies. I am confident that your professional skills, dedication to the traditions established by your predecessors, and clear understanding of the importance of your work will continue to help you successfully implement your plans and ambitious projects, making a significant contribution to enhancing the country’s transport and technological sovereignty.”

articles.title: Commentary by Presidential Aide Yury Ushakov following Vladimir Putin’s telephone conversation with US President Donald Trump
articles.extracted_text: All content on this site is licensed under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International Presidential Aide Yury Ushakov Ushakov Yury Aide to the President : As you know, today, July 4, the United States marks its national holiday, the 250 th Independence Day. The day before, President of Russia Vladimir Putin sent a message of greetings to US President Donald Trump Trump Donald President of the United States of America , expressing confidence that the development of constructive, equal and mutually beneficial ties serves not only the interests of our nations but also of the entire international community. As you may remember, on Russia Day, June 12, the US President sent a message of warm greetings to Vladimir Putin. In that message, Donald Trump paid tribute, and I quote, to the “rich history and culture” of our country and commended the resilience of the Russian people. Of course, today’s conversation between the two heads of state began with President Putin congratulating Donald Trump and the American people on their landmark holiday and recalling Russia’s contribution to the development of American statehood. In this context, the presidents noted the importance of treasuring the historical heritage which our nations share. It was pointed out that our nations will never forget their alliance during World War II. The United States is widely celebrating Independence Day today. This is being done in style and on a grand scale, as Donald Trump knows how to do it, and our President has mentioned this. Their conversation, the fourth one this year, was not only formal but also business-like and constructive. The leaders candidly discussed current issues on the bilateral and international agenda. Incidentally, that conversation lasted nearly an hour and a half, or more precisely, 85 minutes. Naturally, the presidents also talked about a Ukrainian settlement, including in light of Donald Trump’s participation in the upcoming NATO summit in Turkiye on July 7–8. The US President reaffirmed his readiness to facilitate an early termination of the hostilities and the search for peaceful solutions to the crisis. His special envoys – Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner – will carry on their mediation efforts and are ready to come to Moscow at our convenience. The Russian President reaffirmed our commitment to a political and diplomatic settlement of the conflict, with due consideration for Russia’s known principled approaches. However, Kiev and its Western sponsors rely on drawing out and even escalating the conflict and terrorising civilians. Moreover, the European “party of war” proceeds from a false perception of the overall situation and developments on the frontline. Our President has outlined the real situation on the battlefield, where the Russian Armed Forces are confidently advancing, liberating one settlement after another. The establishment of control over one of the Ukrainian Armed Forces’ key fortified settlements, Konstantinovka, is an important stage in the liberation of the Donetsk People’s Republic. No matter how hard the Kiev regime clings to the remaining fortified areas, our army will definitely take them. Regarding the situation around Iran, Vladimir Putin has expressed hope that the negotiation process based on the memorandum of understanding between the United States and Iran will help find mutually acceptable long-term solutions to the key aspects of the settlement. We have confirmed our readiness to provide practical assistance

articles.title: Greetings to President of the United States Donald Trump on Independence Day
articles.raw_summary: Vladimir Putin congratulated President of the United States Donald Trump on the 250 th anniversary of the independence of the United States of America.
articles.extracted_text: All content on this site is licensed under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International Vladimir Putin congratulated President of the United States Donald Trump on the 250 th anniversary of the independence of the United States of America. “The signing of the United States Declaration of Independence not only heralded the birth of your nation but also constituted a landmark event in world history. It was then that Russia extended its unequivocal support to the North American colonists in their struggle for freedom from British rule. Over the course of two and a half centuries, many significant pages have been written in the history of relations between our nations. We fought as allies in two world wars, together liberating humanity from the horrors of Nazism, and subsequently played a pivotal role in shaping the foundations of the contemporary world order. Today, Russia and the United States as the world’s two largest nuclear powers bear a special responsibility for maintaining international security and stability. I am confident that the establishment of constructive, equitable and mutually beneficial relations between Moscow and Washington serves the interests not only of our peoples but also of the entire international community. Donald, I wish you and your loved ones health, well-being and every success, and I wish all citizens of the United States happiness and prosperity.”

articles.title: Greetings on the 80th anniversary of the establishment of the Kaliningrad Region
articles.raw_summary: Vladimir Putin congratulated the residents of the Kaliningrad Region on the 80 th anniversary of its establishment.
articles.extracted_text: All content on this site is licensed under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International Vladimir Putin congratulated the residents of the Kaliningrad Region on the 80 th anniversary of its establishment. “Russia’s westernmost region is rightly renowned for its history, the diversity and beauty of its natural landscapes, and the industriousness, generosity of spirit, and hospitality of its residents. Today, the Kaliningrad Region enjoys considerable potential and offers a wide array of opportunities for attracting substantial investment. Large-scale projects are being successfully implemented across industry and agriculture, transport, construction and infrastructure, tourism, and the spa and resort sector. Crucially, I wish to emphasise that, alongside the launch of modern factories and high-tech production facilities, steadfast attention is paid to preserving the rich cultural and spiritual heritage of the Amber Region, and to implementing vital educational, enlightenment, and creative initiatives. Very recently, the renowned Museum of the World Ocean was awarded the status of a particularly valuable cultural heritage site of the peoples of the Russian Federation. I am confident that your energy, dedication, and sincere love for your native land will continue to help you fulfil your outlined plans.”

articles.title: Visit to the Joint Force auxiliary command post
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Commander-in-Chief visited an auxiliary command post of the Joint Force, where he chaired a meeting with the General Staff leadership and the commanders of the groups of forces.
articles.extracted_text: All content on this site is licensed under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International The Supreme Commander-in-Chief visited an auxiliary command post of the Joint Force, where he chaired a meeting with the General Staff leadership and the commanders of the groups of forces. President of Russia Vladimir Putin: Good afternoon, comrades. Today we are holding a regular working meeting to assess the current situation in the special military operation zone, as well as to review and evaluate the latest combat results. The Chief of the General Staff provides reports on the situation on a nearly daily basis. In this regard, I would like to note that the formations and military units of the Joint Force are carrying out their assigned tasks in accordance with the plans of the General Staff of the Armed Forces. The liberation of the Lugansk People’s Republic has recently been completed in full. Meanwhile, operations to eliminate formations of the Armed Forces of Ukraine in the Donetsk People’s Republic, as well as in the Zaporozhye and Kherson regions, are underway. The establishment of a security zone in the border areas of the Kharkov, Sumy, and Dnepropetrovsk regions of Ukraine is also progressing according to plan. Enemy forces are attempting to maintain their positions, but under the pressure of our troops they are sustaining heavy losses and are being forced to withdraw. Since the beginning of this year, Russian forces have liberated 133 settlements and established control over more than 3,000 square kilometres of our territory in Donbass and Novorossiya. Today’s discussion will focus on developments in the areas of responsibility of the North and South groups of forces. We will also review the results of combat missions carried out by other formations and military units. In addition, during a restricted attendance meeting, as previously agreed with the Chief of the General Staff, we will examine the tasks assigned to the Joint Force for the summer period and discuss the most pressing issues related to supporting ongoing offensive operations. But first, before we proceed to the commanders’ reports, I ask the Chief of the General Staff, in accordance with established procedure, to provide an overview of the general situation in the special military operation zone. Chief of the General Staff of the Russian Armed Forces and First Deputy Minister of Defence Valery Gerasimov Gerasimov Valery Chief of the General Staff of Russia’s Armed Forces – First Deputy Minister of Defence : Comrade Supreme Commander-in-Chief, The troops of the Joint Force continue carrying out their mission during the special military operation to liberate the Donetsk People’s Republic and the Zaporozhye and Kherson regions. In June, 29 settlements and a total of 636 square kilometres of territory were liberated. Unable to achieve results on the battlefield, the Kiev regime is making attempts to persuade its Western sponsors that it has regained the initiative and made significant advances. To achieve this goal, the Kiev regime is conducting an information campaign highlighting the alleged successes of AFU formations while concealing territories liberated by Russian troops by describing them in neutral terms as “moved into the grey zone.” At the same time, units and formations of the Joint Force continue advancing in every operational direction. articles.title: As deadly as war and as powerful as governments, global organized crime remains in the shadows
articles.raw_summary: When people think of the world’s deadliest threats, armed conflicts usually come to mind first. Yet every year, organized crime quietly claims a comparable number of lives.
articles.extracted_text: When people think of the world’s deadliest threats, armed conflicts usually come to mind first. Yet every year, organized crime quietly claims a comparable number of lives. When people think of the world’s deadliest threats, armed conflicts usually come to mind first. Yet every year, organized crime quietly claims a comparable number of lives. Since 2000, the UN estimates that organized criminal groups have been linked to about 95,000 homicides annually. That figure is strikingly close to the average annual death toll from armed conflicts worldwide, estimated at around 92,000 people. The comparison raises an obvious question: if organized crime kills as many people as war, why does it receive far less international attention? As UN Secretary-General António Guterres has warned, “The activities of transnational organized crime take many forms, but the ramifications are the same: weakened governance, corruption and lawlessness, open violence, death and destruction.” Yet beyond these global patterns lies a reality that is often far less visible: The human lives shaped by these networks, and whose experiences rarely appear in headlines or statistics. At 17, Mary left Benin City, Nigeria, believing she was traveling to Europe for a restaurant job and a better future. After transiting through Libya, it became clear that she had been drawn into a trafficking operation. She was subjected to coercion, sexual violence, and exploitation, and found herself unable to contact her family or escape the conditions imposed on her. Looking back on her experience, Mary described the long-term psychological impact.: “What I’m passing through right now is so big, so serious, I see myself as a grown-up. I missed ever being a child.” Yet amid the trauma, she also expressed a fragile sense of possibility, saying, “One day I will have my documents, I will have an education, I will have work.” Her words reflect a broader reality in which survivors often carry both deep trauma and uncertain hope. As another trafficking victim put it, “I often meet girls who dream of going to Turkey and Dubai, to earn more. I tell them, ‘please don’t go. There is nothing good for you there.’” Unlike armed conflicts that unfold openly and dominate global headlines, organized crime operates in the shadows, embedded in communities, economies, and sometimes legitimate institutions. Often, criminal groups do more than generate profits or exploit new technologies; they shape local power structures, influence public life, and at times rival the state itself. According to research by the UN Office on Drugs and Crime (UNODC), organized criminal networks are responsible for roughly one-fifth of all intentional homicides worldwide, rising to about half in parts of the Western Hemisphere. But the toll extends far beyond killings. Drug trafficking drives hundreds of thousands of overdose deaths, including around 600,000 linked to opioids according to the UN’s World Health Organization ( WHO ), while violence against journalists, community leaders, and human rights defenders weakens institutions and erodes public trust. Unlike war, which leaves visible scars that capture international attention, the harm caused by organized crime is often less visible but equally far-reaching. While deaths can be counted, its broader economic, social, and governance costs remain difficult to quantify. articles.title: A safe return home: Tackling stigma on Uganda's Ebola frontline
articles.raw_summary: As Uganda works to contain an Ebola outbreak that is also affecting neighbouring Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), health workers are doing more than responding to a deadly virus. They are helping families navigate fear, misinformation and stigma.
articles.extracted_text: As Uganda works to contain an Ebola outbreak that is also affecting neighbouring Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), health workers are doing more than responding to a deadly virus. They are helping families navigate fear, misinformation and stigma. According to the latest figures, more than 1,400 confirmed cases of Ebola Bundibugyo virus disease, including 350 deaths, have been reported in the two countries. More than 90 per cent of infections are concentrated in Ituri province in DRC, a major cross-border trading hub with Uganda – ravaged by years of armed conflict. On a Sunday morning in Kampala, Dr Chris Opesen, an anthropologist with the World Health Organization ( WHO ), receives a call from local authorities shortly after 5.30am. A woman suspected of having Ebola is preparing to return home, and he has been asked to help ensure her reintegration into the community is safe. Three days earlier, Lilian* had presented with symptoms consistent with Ebola and was transferred to the Ebola isolation unit at Mulago Hospital for diagnosis and care. Although the transfer was planned, it was nonetheless a distressing experience for her, her family and neighbours. Since then, everyone has been anxiously awaiting the final confirmatory test that will determine whether she can safely return home. Over the past 36 hours, Dr Opesen has remained in regular contact with Lilian and her family, offering reassurance and guidance as anxiety in the community has grown. “The anthropologist is the midfielder of the outbreak response,” he says, drawing on a football analogy. “I connect response teams with communities and deliver feedback, from community to response teams citing concerns, fears and grievances.” Together with colleagues from the Kampala Capital City Authority, Dr. Opesen convenes a community meeting with Lilian’s family and friends to address concerns ahead of her return. What begins as a tense and emotional exchange, gradually shifts when Dr. Opesen proposes a more structured dialogue. The group agrees and elects a chairperson, a neighbour, and a secretary: Lilian’s sister, Angela*. As each person is given space to speak, the atmosphere slowly calms. “We appreciate you, because without you there would still be fear,” Angela says afterwards. Later, Dr Opesen and his colleague Henry Bwire, a surveillance focal point with the Kampala Capital City Authority, accompany a neighbour to a local shop. Lilian had asked them to speak to the shopkeeper, who had allegedly stigmatised her mother the previous day. “Ebola is a disease that everyone fears,” Mr. Bwire explains. “Sigma can come through miscommunication and fear, both of which were present in this case. It was our role to bring Lilian back and clear up that miscommunication and reduce that fear in the community.” After the discussion, the shopkeeper thanks the team and reassures them that Lilian will be treated with respect on her return. All that remains is the laboratory confirmation. Both decide to wait on neutral ground rather than return home, positioning themselves between the hospital and Lilian’s neighbourhood. They remain in contact with the laboratory and family throughout the afternoon. At around 6:30pm, the call comes: the result is negative for Ebola. Lilian had been treated for a bacterial infection and is well enough to return home. Back at the house, family and neighbours gather in anticipation. Dr. Opesen arrives with a cake and bottles of water. When Lilian finally arrive

articles.title: Invisible peacebuilders: How ‘diplomacy works’ to prevent conflict
articles.raw_summary: Described as “sometimes modest, sometimes historic,” UN special political missions have quietly been preventing the escalation of conflicts during the organization’s 80-year history, becoming a key instrument for maintaining peace.
articles.extracted_text: Described as “sometimes modest, sometimes historic,” UN special political missions have quietly been preventing the escalation of conflicts during the organization’s 80-year history, becoming a key instrument for maintaining peace. Many of the United Nations' biggest successes are the crises that never make the headlines. Around the world, special political missions work quietly to ease tensions, broker agreements and support fragile political transitions. Their tools are negotiation, mediation and diplomacy. Unlike the perhaps more visible peacekeeping missions, they have no armoured vehicles or armed troops. At the launch of the first-ever comprehensive overview of these missions , Rosemary DiCarlo, UN Under-Secretary-General for Political and Peacebuilding Affairs, described their record as “sometimes modest, sometimes historic,” adding that it points to “one enduring truth: diplomacy works.” That lesson, she said, is especially relevant today. The review covers the period from 1948 to 2025 and shows how the Organization’s political role has evolved alongside a changing world. The first such mission was established in May 1948 almost immediately after the United Nations was created. Swedish diplomat Count Folke Bernadotte was appointed as the UN Mediator in Palestine, the first time the UN deployed a special envoy to help resolve an armed conflict. Today, special political missions perform a wide range of functions. Some facilitate peace negotiations, others monitor ceasefire agreements, support border demarcation, investigate serious violations, or assist political reform processes. According to Rosemary DiCarlo, the defining feature of special political missions is their diversity. “They have taken the form of envoys of the Secretary-General, of fact finding and investigative missions, of regional offices, of panels of experts helping the Security Council monitor sanctions regimes, and of missions accompanying complex political transitions,” she said. “Flexibility has always been their strength. The same instrument that helped broker a ceasefire can also demarcate a border or support the dismantling of a chemical weapons program. Few multilateral instruments are as adaptable,” she explained. One of the most remarkable early examples was the UN’s role in Libya’s path to independence. By the late 1940s, the country, which had been an Italian colony from 1911 to 1942 and before that part of the Ottoman Empire, was divided and operated under different administrative systems. A UN commission helped bridge political differences, draft a constitution, establish a provisional government, create a unified financial system and train civil servants. Just two years later, Libya became the first country to achieve independence through a UN sponsored process. Similar missions supported decolonization elsewhere. UN representatives organized plebiscites and referendums in Cameroon, Equatorial Guinea and Togoland, conducted consultations with the people of Bahrain and helped newly independent states build their own institutions. During the Cold War, the Security Council’s ability to act was often constrained by rivalry between the superpowers. As a result, the United Nations increasingly relied on the Secretary-General’s special representatives. In the 1980s, the Secretary-General’s Personal Representative led years of negotiations on Afghanistan that culminated in the signing of the Geneva Accords in 1988. articles.title: Press Release
Souvenir Items
Research Scheme
articles.extracted_text: RAJYA SABHA RESEARCH AND STUDY (RSRS) SCHEME The Rajya Sabha instituted the Dr. S. Radhakrishnan Chair and two Rajya Sabha Fellowships in the year 2009 with the objective of promoting research on different aspects of parliamentary democracy in India. The Scheme has been revamped in 2019 and again in 2023 in order to enhance its effectiveness and utility. The broad objective of the Scheme is ‘to promote research/empirical/comparative study of the functioning of Parliament and other democratic institutions and analyse their contributions to the country’s socio-economic transformation and to ensure the utility of the research study to the functioning of Parliament’. The Rajya Sabha Internship Programme is nomination-based. The programme aims to bring the functioning of Parliament closer to the youth by providing them an opportunity to familiarize themselves with the overall functioning of Parliament at the macro level and the Rajya Sabha Secretariat at the micro level. Director (Research), Rajya Sabha Secretariat
